tossups.question: In a dramatic adaptation, a retired member of this group sneaks into a squire’s mansion with his pearl-wearing daughter to kill everyone. A craze for depicting the members of this group was spurred by a print series that launched a full-body tattoo fad alongside the artist Utagawa Kuniyoshi’s career. These characters, who are ranked by a tablet in the Loyalty Hall, emerge in the form of golden rays from a black cloud when a stone (*) tortoise is unearthed. In a novel attributed to Shi Nai’an in which these characters are divided between Heavenly Spirits and Earthly Fiends, they oppose Gao Qiu and operate under Song Jiang out of Mount Liang and the nearby boglands For 10 points, identify this set of one hundred and eight heroes from a Classic Chinese Novel.

A craze for depicting the members of this group was spurred by a print series that launched a full-body tattoo fad alongside the artist Utagawa Kuniyoshi's career. These characters, who are ranked by a tablet in the Loyalty Hall, emerge in the form of golden rays from a black cloud when a stone (*) tortoise is unearthed. In a novel attributed to Shi Nai'an in which these characters are divided between Heavenly Spirits and Earthly Fiends, they oppose Gao Qiu and operate under Song Jiang out of Mount Liang and the nearby boglands For 10 points, identify this set of one hundred and eight heroes from a Classic Chinese Novel.
tossups.answer: the outlaws of the marsh [or 108 Stars of Destiny; or the Suikoden; or any answers about heroes or bandits from Water Margin, Outlaws of the Marsh, All Men Are Brothers, The Marshes of Mount Liang, Shui Hu Zhuan, or Suikoden; accept 72 Earthly Fiends or Earthly Stars or 36 Heavenly Spirits or Heavenly Stars before “Heavenly Spirits” is read]

packet.name: A – ASU A, Berkeley A, Maryland A, WUSTL B

tossups.question: A couple in this novel who are nicknamed “the Ogress” and “the gardener” run an inn where guests are killed and used as meat filling for food. In this novel, a character is tattooed on the face instead of being executed for murdering his wife. A character in this novel sees a sign warning not to cross a ridge after drinking three bowls of wine, but instead drinks 18 bowls of wine and punches a tiger unconscious. That character, Wǔ Sōng, inspired a spinoff of this novel titled The Plum in the Golden Vase. In this novel, Gāo Qiú (“ch’yoh”) pursues the “Stars of Destiny.” For 10 points, name this Chinese classical novel by Shī Nài’ān (“shurr nye-AHN”) in which Sòng Jiāng leads a group of 108 bandits.
tossups.answer: Water Margin [or Shuǐ Hǔ Zhuàn; or Outlaws of the Marsh; or Tale of the Marshes; or All Men Are Brothers]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Water Margin [or Shui Hu Zhuan; or Outlaws of the Marsh; or Tale of the Marshes; or All Men Are Brothers]
tossups.question_sanitized: A couple in this novel who are nicknamed "the Ogress" and "the gardener" run an inn where guests are killed and used as meat filling for food. That character, Wu Song, inspired a spinoff of this novel titled The Plum in the Golden Vase. In this novel, Gao Qiu ("ch'yoh") pursues the "Stars of Destiny." For 10 points, name this Chinese classical novel by Shi Nai'an ("shurr nye-AHN") in which Song Jiang leads a group of 108 bandits.

tossups.question: After he’s caught eating beef without permission, a character in this novel is cursed to walk at superhuman speed. An antagonist in this novel rises from a street urchin to a marshal after impressing a prince with his football skills. This novel begins with a black cloud splitting into a hundred golden rays after the excavation of a stone tortoise. A spin-off of this novel features a minor character whose pursuit of women leads to his death from (*) aphrodisiac overdose. The protagonist of the erotic novel Plum in the Golden Vase is killed by the “tiger-slayer” Wu Song in this novel, which also features characters like the “Black Whirlwind” and focuses on a band of characters led by Song Jiang. For 10 points, name this classical Chinese novel written by Shi Nai’an about the 108 “Stars of Destiny.”
tossups.answer: Water Margin [or Outlaws of the March; or All Men Are Brothers; or Shui Hu Zhuan]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Water Margin [or Outlaws of the March; or All Men Are Brothers; or Shui Hu Zhuan]

For 10 points, name this classical Chinese novel written by Shi Nai'an about the 108 "Stars of Destiny."

tossups.question: One character in this novel is offered one hundred gold pieces, only accepting one, and then murders his mistress when she demands all one hundred. Another character in this novel leaves his blind mother to fetch water for her, then returns to discover that she has been eaten. Later chapters of this novel diverge in its “simple” and “complex” editions. A character in this novel who drinks 18 bowls of wine, then beats a (*) tiger to death, later appears in the sexually graphic novel The Plum in the Golden Vase. After being appointed Grand Marshal, the villainous Gāo Qiú attempts to eliminate this novel’s central group, which is made up of “Earthly Fiends” and “Heavenly Spirits.” For 10 points, name this 14th-century Chinese novel that follows a group of 108 bandits known as the Stars of Destiny.
tossups.answer: The Water Margin [or Shuǐ Hǔ Zhuàn or Outlaws of the Marsh or All Men Are Brothers]
tossups.answer_sanitized: The Water Margin [or Shui Hu Zhuan or Outlaws of the Marsh or All Men Are Brothers]
tossups.question_sanitized: One character in this novel is offered one hundred gold pieces, only accepting one, and then murders his mistress when she demands all one hundred. Another character in this novel leaves his blind mother to fetch water for her, then returns to discover that she has been eaten. Later chapters of this novel diverge in its "simple" and "complex" editions. A character in this novel who drinks 18 bowls of wine, then beats a (*) tiger to death, later appears in the sexually graphic novel The Plum in the Golden Vase. After being appointed Grand Marshal, the villainous Gao Qiu attempts to eliminate this novel's central group, which is made up of "Earthly Fiends" and "Heavenly Spirits." For 10 points, name this 14th-century Chinese novel that follows a group of 108 bandits known as the Stars of Destiny.

tossups.question: After his hut collapses under heavy snowfall, a character in this novel takes refuge in a temple whose deity he pleases with the severed heads of two enemies. Sidney Shapiro’s acclaimed translation of this novel amalgamates the 70- and 100-chapter “complex” editions, which diverge at the point where the main characters are granted amnesty. At the start of this novel, its main characters shoot out in a black cloud upon being released from a stone statue of a tortoise. After consuming eighteen bowls of wine, a character in this novel pummels a tiger to death with his bare fists. In this novel, a set of characters with nicknames like “Black Whirlwind” and “Pilgrim” capture their nemesis Gao Qiu (“gow chyoh”) during the fifth imperial siege of their hideout on Mount Liang. For 10 points, name this Classical Chinese novel about the adventures of the “Stars of Destiny,” a group of 108 bandits.
tossups.answer: Water Margin [or Shui Hu Zhuan or Outlaws of the Marsh or Tale of the Marshes or Men of the Marshes; or All Men Are Brothers; accept The Marshes of Mount Liang before “Liang”]

tossups.question: Only three of these characters are women, one of whom owns the Black Inn where some of them meet. One of these people is a monk with a heavy staff who is nicknamed for his flowery tattoos, while another dies of poisoned wine and is persuaded by a mother to make a concubine of her daughter (*) Yan Poxi. Chao Gai is the first leader of these people, who oppose the corrupt official Gao Qiu and are called the 108 Stars of Destiny. Mount Liang is the base of - for 10 points - what group of people led by Song Jiang in a classic Chinese novel?
tossups.answer: the outlaws (from Water Margin) [or the Outlaws of the Marsh; accept any term loosely equivalent to “outlaw” such as brigands or bandits or highwaymen; accept anything mentioning Song Jiang and his companions until mentioned; accept more specific answers referring to the group, such as 108 Stars of Destiny or 72 Earthly Fiends or 36 Heavenly Stars until “Stars of Destiny” is read]

tossups.question: Assholes in this novel include a pair of tavern owners that drug their customers, murder them and serve their flesh in buns. In chapter 26, a coroner steals bones from a funeral pyre to prove that a man was poisoned. After being poisoned, the protagonist of this novel dreams that the “Tower-Shifting Heavenly King” told him that an “earth fiend star” south of a river can cure him. Its protagonist kills his concubine (*) Yan Poxi when she attempts to blackmail him. That character, Song Jiang, was released from a stone tortoise and opposes the corrupt official Gao Qiu, who seeks to root out the “Stars of Destiny.” For 10 points, identify this novel named after the 108 bandits based in Liangshan, one of the four great classical novels of China.
tossups.answer: Water Margin [Accept Shui Hu Zhuan or Outlaws of the Marsh or All Men Are Brothers]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Water Margin [Accept Shui Hu Zhuan or Outlaws of the Marsh or All Men Are Brothers]

tossups.question: Utagawa Kuniyoshi’s woodblock illustrations of this novel inspired a craze for full-body tattooing. Translated into English by Sidney Shapiro, this novel includes a character who bashes a tiger to death with his bare hands, who appears in an episode that became the basis for The Plum in the Golden Vase. Its characters include the physician An Daoquan, a messenger who ties Taoist talismans on his legs to magically travel vast distances, and a warrior nicknamed “Black Whirlwind.” This novel ends with its main characters fighting the forces of Fang La and beating back an invasion by the Liao Dynasty. Attributed to Shi Nai’an, it centers on the conflict between Gao Qiu and Song Jiang, who leads a group based in Liangshan. For 10 points, name this classic Chinese novel about the advneutres of one hundred and eight bandits.
tossups.answer: Outlaws of the Marsh [or Water Margin; or All Men Are Brothers; or Shui Hu Zhuan]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Outlaws of the Marsh [or Water Margin; or All Men Are Brothers; or Shui Hu Zhuan]

The Upanishads, Brahma Sutras, Maha Puranas, and Mahabharata are all attributed to this legendary sage, who narrated them while Ganesha wrote them down.

The Muktika Upanishad includes a canon of this many Upanishads, the number of Gopis of Vrindavan. While visiting a temple to find Celestial Master Zhang, Marshal Hong accidentally releases demons totaling this auspicious number.

Vyasa [or Vedavyasa; or Krishna Dvaipayana]
108 (the last sentence refers to the 108 Stars of Destiny from Water Margin)

Water Margin [or Outlaws of the Marsh; or Men of the Marshes or Tale of the Marshes or Shuihu Zhuan or Suikoden; accept All Men Are Brothers; accept The Marshes of Mount Liang]

bonuses.leadin_sanitized: After Prince Duan ("dwahn") becomes emperor, this character is appointed as the Grand Marshal and subsequently abuses his power to exact revenge on those who had previously wronged him. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this character who Lin Chong suggests killing after he is captured by the Stars of Destiny during a siege on Mount Liang.
Gao Qiu is an antagonist of this classic Chinese novel about a group of bandits led by Song Jiang.
This is the number of bandits, or Stars of Destiny, in Water Margin.

bonuses.leadin: In one of this author’s novels, Xu Sanguan (“shoo san-gwen”) discovers that his first son, Yile (“yee-luh”), is illegitimate. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author of Chronicle of a Blood Merchant. He wrote a novel about Xu Fugui (“shoo foo-gway”), who survives the Cultural Revolution after gambling away his father’s fortune in To Live.
This poet “translated” a poem by Li Bai that begins while the speaker’s “hair was still cut straight across [her] forehead”; that poem is “The River Merchant’s Wife.”

In The Plum in the Golden Vase, Ximen Qing (“shee-mun cheeng”) is a wealthy merchant who first appeared in this other classical Chinese novel, about Song Jiang (“sung j’yong”) and his band of 108 outlaws.
answers: Yu Hua [or Hua Yu]
Ezra Pound
Water Margin [or Shui Hu Zhuan; or All Men Are Brothers; or Outlaws of the Marsh; or Tale of the Marshes; or Men of the Marshes; or The Marshes of Mount Liang]

parts_sanitized: Name this author of Chronicle of a Blood Merchant. He wrote a novel about Xu Fugui ("shoo foo-gway"), who survives the Cultural Revolution after gambling away his father's fortune in To Live.

In The Plum in the Golden Vase, Ximen Qing ("shee-mun cheeng") is a wealthy merchant who first appeared in this other classical Chinese novel, about Song Jiang ("sung j'yong") and his band of 108 outlaws.

bonuses.leadin: The protagonist of the novel The Carnal Prayer Mat, Weiyangsheng (“way-yong-shung”), is a man with this occupation who has a magician splice strips of a dog’s penis onto his own to enhance its power. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this occupation that The Carnal Prayer Mat is alternately titled for a “midnight” example of. A loosely structured comic novel by Wu Jingzi (“woo jing-dzuh”) follows several people of this occupation.

Novels satirizing scholars, such as those of the “talent and beauty” genre, became popular during this final ruling Chinese dynasty. Writers such as Lu Xun (“loo shwun”) wrote in the aftermath of this dynasty’s collapse in the 1910s.
This novel’s characters include Xiao Rang (“sh’yao rong”), whose nickname is “Sacred Handed Scholar.” Pearl Buck made a bad translation of this novel under the title All Men Are Brothers.
answers: scholars [accept The Scholars or The Midnight Scholar or literati or shì; prompt on intellectuals or academics]
Qing dynasty [or Manchu dynasty]
Water Margin [or Outlaws of the Marsh or The Marshes of Mount Liang or Shuǐhǔ Zhuàn]
answers_sanitized: scholars [accept The Scholars or The Midnight Scholar or literati or shi; prompt on intellectuals or academics]
Qing dynasty [or Manchu dynasty]
Water Margin [or Outlaws of the Marsh or The Marshes of Mount Liang or Shuihu Zhuan]

parts_sanitized: Give this occupation that The Carnal Prayer Mat is alternately titled for a "midnight" example of. A loosely structured comic novel by Wu Jingzi ("woo jing-dzuh") follows several people of this occupation.

Novels satirizing scholars, such as those of the "talent and beauty" genre, became popular during this final ruling Chinese dynasty. Writers such as Lu Xun ("loo shwun") wrote in the aftermath of this dynasty's collapse in the 1910s.
This novel's characters include Xiao Rang ("sh'yao rong"), whose nickname is "Sacred Handed Scholar." Pearl Buck made a bad translation of this novel under the title All Men Are Brothers.

bonuses.leadin: Arthur Waley argued that the Gong’an school painter Xu Wei was perhaps the most likely candidate for this book’s author For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this novel centred on Ximen Qing much of whose text centres on a series of 72 sexual incidents. Its Chinese title combined the name of three of its female characters.
The Plum in the Golden Vase, perhaps written by Xu Wei, was based on this Chinese novel and shares several of its character including Wu Song who kills a tiger with his bare hands.

Xu Wei did actually write four plays, one of which is titled for [this character] who goes to War in Her Father's Place. This character makes the decision to be conscripted because her father is very elderly and her brother is a child.
answers: Plum in the Golden Vase (accept Golden Lotus; accept Jin Ping Mei)
Water Margin
Hua Mulan
answers_sanitized: Plum in the Golden Vase (accept Golden Lotus; accept Jin Ping Mei)
Water Margin
Hua Mulan
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Arthur Waley argued that the Gong'an school painter Xu Wei was perhaps the most likely candidate for this book's author For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Identify this novel centred on Ximen Qing much of whose text centres on a series of 72 sexual incidents. Its Chinese title combined the name of three of its female characters.

Xu Wei did actually write four plays, one of which is titled for [this character] who goes to War in Her Father's Place. This character makes the decision to be conscripted because her father is very elderly and her brother is a child.

bonuses.leadin: At the start of this novel, Marshal Hong releases 108 stars of destiny, imprisoned under an ancient stele-bearing tortoise, which are re-born as heroes. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this classic Chinese novel about a group of outlaws who gather at the Liangshan marsh.

This principal antagonist of Water Margin is a corrupt minister of the Huizong emperor. He frames Lin Chong for attempted assassination and tries to have him killed by bribing the guards taking him into exile.

The most highly regarded translation of Water Margin was written by this Jewish-American who died in 2014. This author of the autobiography “I Chose China” became a naturalized Chinese citizen in 1963.
answers: Water Margin (accept All Men Are Brothers or Heroes of the Marsh or Shui Hu Zhuan)
Gao Qiu (accept Kao Chou)
Sidney Shapiro
answers_sanitized: Water Margin (accept All Men Are Brothers or Heroes of the Marsh or Shui Hu Zhuan)
Gao Qiu (accept Kao Chou)
Sidney Shapiro
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: At the start of this novel, Marshal Hong releases 108 stars of destiny, imprisoned under an ancient stele-bearing tortoise, which are re-born as heroes. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this classic Chinese novel about a group of outlaws who gather at the Liangshan marsh.

This principal antagonist of Water Margin is a corrupt minister of the Huizong emperor. He frames Lin Chong for attempted assassination and tries to have him killed by bribing the guards taking him into exile.

The most highly regarded translation of Water Margin was written by this Jewish-American who died in 2014. This author of the autobiography "I Chose China" became a naturalized Chinese citizen in 1963.

bonuses.leadin: Wu Hsing-kuo turned this novel into a Peking Rock Opera named after its title group of outlaws. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this member of the “Four Great Classical Novels” of Chinese literature. In this work, Marshal Hong releases a group of spirits to fight the corrupt Song dynasty, including Song Jiang, who opposes Gao Qiu.

Hsing-kuo’s opera is titled for this number of heroes. This is the number of outlaws that gathers at Liangshan Marsh, and this many spirits are released from a tortoise in the first scene of the novel.

Wu Yong, Chao Gai, and other outlaws accomplished this task by disguising themselves as date traders and pretending to drink drugged wine before giving it to a convoy, who falls asleep so this action can be accomplished.
answers: Water Margin
stealing the birthday gifts of Imperial Tutor Chai Jing [or equivalents like depriving Chai Jing’s of his birthday gifts]
answers_sanitized: Water Margin
stealing the birthday gifts of Imperial Tutor Chai Jing [or equivalents like depriving Chai Jing's of his birthday gifts]

Hsing-kuo's opera is titled for this number of heroes. This is the number of outlaws that gathers at Liangshan Marsh, and this many spirits are released from a tortoise in the first scene of the novel.

Wu Yong, Chao Gai, and other outlaws accomplished this task by disguising themselves as date traders and pretending to drink drugged wine before giving it to a convoy, who falls asleep so this action can be accomplished.

bonuses.leadin: An author with this surname wrote about Mumbai’s Parsi community in Chronicle of a Corpse Bearer. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this surname of an author of a novel in which the brothers Narayan and Ishvar work as tailors in Dina’s flat during their country’s turbulent 1970s.

Rohinton Mistry’s A Delicate Balance is set during the rule of a person with this surname. A man with this surname discussed his methods of “peaceful resistance” and Satyagraha in The Story of My Experiments with Truth.
Mohandas Gandhi’s writings were also collected in a book titled All Men Are Brothers, a phrase that has also been used to title a translation of this novel about 108 bandits who gather on a mountain.
answers: Mistry [or Rohinton Mistry; or Cyrus Mistry]
Gandhi
Water Margin [or Outlaws of the Marsh; or Shuǐ hǔ zhuàn; or anything mentioning a tale about a Marsh or Mount Liáng]
answers_sanitized: Mistry [or Rohinton Mistry; or Cyrus Mistry]
Gandhi
Water Margin [or Outlaws of the Marsh; or Shui hu zhuan; or anything mentioning a tale about a Marsh or Mount Liang]

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about Chinese erotic novels, for 10 points each.
parts: This novel by Wei Hui, which was banned in China, is about a writer named Nikki, also called Coco after Coco Chanel, who writes a collection of erotic short stories called The Shriek of the Butterfly.
The classic novel The Plum in the Golden Vase is about people like Ximen Qing and Pan Jinlian, who are minor characters from this other novel about Song Jian and his band of 108 criminals.

The Qing author Li Yu wrote a novel named after a “carnal” one of these objects. Its title comes from a comparison the protagonist makes between his carnal desires and the loftier desires of a monk he meets.
answers: Shanghai Baby
The Water Margin [or:Outlaws of the Marsh, All Men Are Brothers, The Marshes of Mount Liang, Tale of the Marshes, Men of the Marshes, Shui Hu Zhuan]
prayer mat [or:zafu]
answers_sanitized: Shanghai Baby
The Water Margin [or:Outlaws of the Marsh, All Men Are Brothers, The Marshes of Mount Liang, Tale of the Marshes, Men of the Marshes, Shui Hu Zhuan]
prayer mat [or:zafu]

bonuses.leadin: This novel begins with the release of 108 Stars of Destiny, which are re-born as heroes such as Lin Chong and Wu Song. For 10 points each:
parts: This is which first of the Four Great Classical Novels of Chinese literature, in which the heroes assemble in the Liangshan marshes to fight the corrupt Song government of Gao Qiu [“gow chioh”] and later its enemies?

In Water Margin, Wu Song slays his murdered brother’s wife and her lover. But this later novel, these characters marry, and Ximen Qing [“shee men ching”] dies from sexual excess before Wu Song returns to kill his wife. This novel is now often considered the fifth Great Classical Novel
Meaning ‘Golden Lotus’, this is the name of the wife of Wu Dalang and then Ximen Qing in Water Margin and The Plum in the Golden Vase
answers: Water Margin (or Outlaws of the Marsh, Tale of the Marshes, All Men Are Brothers, Men of the Marshes, The Marshes of Mount Liang or Shui Hu Zhuan)
The Plum in the Golden Vase (or The Golden Lotus or Jin Ping Mei)
Pan Jinlian (or P’an Chin Lien)
answers_sanitized: Water Margin (or Outlaws of the Marsh, Tale of the Marshes, All Men Are Brothers, Men of the Marshes, The Marshes of Mount Liang or Shui Hu Zhuan)
The Plum in the Golden Vase (or The Golden Lotus or Jin Ping Mei)
Pan Jinlian (or P'an Chin Lien)

parts_sanitized: This is which first of the Four Great Classical Novels of Chinese literature, in which the heroes assemble in the Liangshan marshes to fight the corrupt Song government of Gao Qiu ["gow chioh"] and later its enemies?

In Water Margin, Wu Song slays his murdered brother's wife and her lover. But this later novel, these characters marry, and Ximen Qing ["shee men ching"] dies from sexual excess before Wu Song returns to kill his wife. This novel is now often considered the fifth Great Classical Novel
Meaning 'Golden Lotus', this is the name of the wife of Wu Dalang and then Ximen Qing in Water Margin and The Plum in the Golden Vase

bonuses.leadin: Identify some Chinese novels, for 10 points each.
parts: This novel follows a Buddhist monk, Tripitaka who tries to bring Buddhism to China. He is joined by Sandy, Pigsy and the Monkey King.

This one-hundred chapter novel by Shi Nai’an is based on the exploits of the outlaw Song Jiang and his companions during the Song dynasty. It sees the one-hundred-and-eight stars of destiny fight the forces of Gao Qiu.

Tris Cole wants you to know that this novel uses several themes from the Water Margin although it is better known for its depiction of sexual fetishes. The sex could get a bit dangerous as the concubine Li Ping’er bleeds to death and the protagonist Ximen Qing dies after an overdose of aphrodisiacs.
answers: Journey to the West [or Xī Yóu Jì; or Hsi-yu chi; prompt on “Monkey”, which is an abridged translation]
Water Margin [or Shuǐ Hǔ Zhuàn; or Outlaws of the Marsh and equivalent translations; or All Men Are Brothers; or The Marshes of Mount Liang]
The Plum in the Golden Vase [accept equivalent translations; or Jin Ping Mei; or The Golden Lotus]
answers_sanitized: Journey to the West [or Xi You Ji; or Hsi-yu chi; prompt on "Monkey", which is an abridged translation]
Water Margin [or Shui Hu Zhuan; or Outlaws of the Marsh and equivalent translations; or All Men Are Brothers; or The Marshes of Mount Liang]
The Plum in the Golden Vase [accept equivalent translations; or Jin Ping Mei; or The Golden Lotus]

This one-hundred chapter novel by Shi Nai'an is based on the exploits of the outlaw Song Jiang and his companions during the Song dynasty. It sees the one-hundred-and-eight stars of destiny fight the forces of Gao Qiu.

Tris Cole wants you to know that this novel uses several themes from the Water Margin although it is better known for its depiction of sexual fetishes. The sex could get a bit dangerous as the concubine Li Ping'er bleeds to death and the protagonist Ximen Qing dies after an overdose of aphrodisiacs.

bonuses.leadin: This character’s lef arm is lopped of by a magic sword, and he receives his nickname by donning the disguise of a Buddhist monk afer he leaves a bloody note acknowledging his slaughter of Inspector Zhang. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this powerful man who kills his brother’s murderer Pan Jianlin in the novel The Golden Lotus. In another novel, he becomes excessively drunk and punches a tiger to death.
Wu Song is one of the 108 bandits from this classic Chinese novel.
This mountain, which also appears in the Yuan Dynasty play Black Hurricane Presents Twin Victories, is home to the stronghold of the 108 bandits in Water Margin.
answers: <u>Wu Song</u> [or <u>Pilgrim</u>; prompt on partial name]
<u>Water Margin</u> [or <u>Shui Hu</u> Zhuan; or <u>All Men are Brothers</u>; or <u>Outlaws of the Marsh</u>; or close approximat<u>es and synonyms o</u>f th<u>ose answer</u>s]
Mount <u>Liang</u> [or <u>Liangshan</u>]
answers_sanitized: Wu Song [or Pilgrim; prompt on partial name]
Water Margin [or Shui Hu Zhuan; or All Men are Brothers; or Outlaws of the Marsh; or close approximates and synonyms of those answers]
Mount Liang [or Liangshan]
